scope_check.evidence: Reviewed outputs are within the assigned handoff board app scope.

goal_contract_check.evidence: The implementation matches the shift handoff Goal Contract, including target users, workflows, data entities, and state requirements.

question_fulfillment.evidence: The first-screen handoff board satisfies the requested app experience for charge nurse shift handoffs.

functional_completeness.evidence: Create, edit, complete, filter, validation, and local state transitions are implemented.

scenario_flow_coverage.evidence: Both priority handoff creation and unresolved-task filtering flows passed with edge cases.

edge_case_handling.evidence: Empty, loading, validation, disabled, saved, and recoverable error states were exercised.

regression_safety.evidence: Focused behavior test covers the main workflow and count updates.

verification_completeness.evidence: Build, test, run, desktop/mobile visual checks, accessibility basics, and state coverage evidence are recorded.

scenario_flow_scores.evidence: Created room 412B handoff with critical readiness, alert summary, and one open task; saved card appeared in priority lane and invalid missing room disabled save.
Filtered unresolved tasks, opened the details area, completed one task, and observed card count and readiness labels update before clearing the filter.

build.evidence: `npm run build` completed with production assets generated.

test.evidence: `npm test -- handoff-flow` passed the create, filter, and complete-task workflow test.

run.evidence: Local dev server opened at http://localhost:5173 and rendered the shift handoff board.

behavior_check.evidence: Manual behavior check created a fictional patient handoff, filtered unresolved tasks, completed a task, triggered validation, and reset an empty filter state.

ux_workflow_completeness.evidence: The first screen contains the handoff board, priority filters, add/edit form, and task completion controls needed for the primary shift handoff workflow.

visual_polish.evidence: Desktop and mobile inspections show clear hierarchy for acuity, readiness, alerts, and task counts with deliberate spacing and non-generic clinical labels.

responsive_desktop_mobile.evidence: Desktop 1440x900 uses a multi-column board; mobile 390x844 stacks cards and wraps actions without overlap or clipped alert text.

accessibility_basics.evidence: Keyboard tab order reaches filters, form fields, save, edit, and task controls; visible focus, labels, aria-live status text, and contrast-sensitive priority badges were inspected.

error_loading_empty_states.evidence: Loading seed data, empty filter result, validation errors, disabled invalid save, save success message, and simulated local state error were all visible and recoverable.

text_overlap_layout_stability.evidence: Long room labels, assigned nurse names, and alert text wrapped inside cards at 1440x900 and 390x844 without moving controls or overlapping neighboring content.

domain_fit.evidence: Sample data and labels use fictional clinical handoff terms: room, acuity, assigned nurse, priority alert, open care task, and readiness status.

evidence.description: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot inspected: handoff board, priority filters, add form, and details card are visible with no overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot inspected: stacked cards, wrapped alert text, and reachable add/save controls remain usable.
Primary workflow behavior check: created a room 412B handoff, filtered unresolved tasks, completed one task, and confirmed counts updated.
Accessibility basics check: labels, visible focus, keyboard operation, aria-live save status, and contrast of readiness badges inspected.
State coverage test exercised empty filter results, loading seed data, recoverable error, saved-success message, disabled invalid save, and validation messages.

security_check.evidence: The prototype uses fictional sample records and no external services, credentials, or protected health information.

secret_scan_result.evidence: Secret scan found no API keys or credential-like values in changed files.

scope_diff_result.evidence: Changed files matched the assigned app implementation and test scope.

command_audit.purpose: Verify primary workflow behavior and state coverage.

risks_or_follow_up: The prototype is local-only and must not be used with real patient data.
